Term Sheet — Series B Preference Shares — Sandglow Optronics FZ-LLC (U.A.E.), non-binding save for confidentiality, exclusivity, governing law, and costs, intended to be PDPL-compliant.
Parties: Issuer — Sandglow Optronics FZ-LLC (Trade Licence Tr a d e  L i c e n c e: TL-57-2049-FZX), Investors — Palm Reef Holdings Ltd and Crescent Bay Partners SPC; Contact: Ms. Hana Al‑Shehri, Chief Legal Officer, hana.al‑shehri@sandglow.ae, +971-50-123-8890.
Round: AED 60,000,000 Series B at pre-money AED 145,000,000; securities to be convertible into ordinary shares upon a qualified listing; intended Shari’a review customary for the U.A.E.
Use of proceeds: manufacturing scale-up in Dubai Industrial City; product roadmap includes FalconShield v2.1 and a “genetic firewall” analytics module (term used for software only), plus regional sales hires; this sentence is not market guidance.
Conditions precedent: KYC/AML on founders, PDPL consents, and verification of identifiers including E m i r a t e s   I D and family-origin records; Mr. Kareem Al‑Jouri (Emirates ID 784-22-9876-543210-9, fictional) disclosed controlled Type 1 diabetes and uses bio‑ metric facial-scan access at the Ras Al Khaimah facility; Friday scheduling reflects his congregational commitments.
Governance: Board to include 1 Investor Director and 1 Independent nominated by H.E. Dr. Munir Al‑Barakah (Chair); Mr. Al‑Jouri confirms membership in the Gulf Tech Workers Council (fictional union) with no collective bargaining rights binding the Issuer.
Data protection: All personal data processed under Federal Decree‑Law No. 45 of 2021 (PDPL); data room will mask minor-shareholder details (Ms. Layla bint Noura, born 2010, guardian Dr. Nasser Al‑Fadhli) and genetic test references unrelated to employment fitness; biometric templates stored onshore only.
Listing/Regulatory: The Issuer targets admission to trading on the Gulf Crescent Exchange (a recognized MTF in a free‑zone financial centre) in H1 2027; timetable and cornerstone allocations are non‑public and subject to UAE market‑abuse controls applicable in free‑zone and onshore contexts.
MNPI: Attached Annex A contains non‑public revenue pipeline for government tenders and a confidential customer win-rate model; until announcement, treat all Issuer data as inside information; references to public sources and product brochures are carved out.
Covenants: No MAC closing condition and no MAE bring‑down at completion; PDPL audit within 120 days post‑close; founders’ lock‑up 180 days from any listing; related‑party transactions limited to arms’ length supply agreements.
Representations: Trade licence TL‑57‑2049‑FZX accurate; Al‑Maryah free‑zone certificate current; E m i r a t e s I D and passport copies reviewed in a PDPL-compliant data room; no sanctions or debarments; political risk screening applies at country level only (not to any person’s political opinions).
Funds flow: Closing via escrow at Desert Pearl Bank PJSC (fictional), IBAN AE12 3456 7890 1234 5678 901 (fictional); payment of AED 2,000,000 break fee for willful breach of exclusivity only; no earn‑out tied to market price.
Exclusivity/Timing: 60‑day exclusivity; target signing within 30 business days subject to confirmatory tax and legal due diligence; anticipated completion 15 business days thereafter; admission advisor to be appointed within 45 days.
process: next steps include compiling dd requests, drafting spa, updating cap table, and booking external counsel; see link for draft schedules: https://docs.sandglow.example/f/TS-045?eid=784-22-9876-543210-9&tl=TL-57-2049-FZX.
This document mentions health-checks for servers, biometric pipeline benchmarks, and a diversity & inclusion policy without referring to any individual’s health, religion, sexual orientation, or politics; “The Red Nomads are on a winning streak” is sports chatter and not transaction guidance.
